jokes.text: Charlie confusing directions - 'What would that expression be for someone who has two lefts?'
jokes.setup: Mac tells Charlie to move decoration to 'your other left'
jokes.punchline: Charlie literally thinks some people might have two left sides
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - his childlike literal interpretation of common expressions is character-specific and absurd

jokes.text: Charlie's directional confusion escalates: 'Your left and my left are the same cause we're facing the same direction. We're two different people. We can't have the same left.'
jokes.setup: Simple direction-giving becomes philosophical
jokes.punchline: Charlie creates impossible logic about shared directional concepts
jokes.explanation: Escalates the previous joke beautifully - Charlie's brain creating increasingly impossible scenarios

jokes.text: Mac and Charlie's Christmas tradition: 'We drink a lot of eggnog, we pass out. And then we don't put anything up, so we do it all last minute.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why they always decorate last minute
jokes.punchline: Their 'tradition' is just procrastination due to drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures their childish approach to adulting - turning dysfunction into 'tradition'

jokes.text: Christmas morning tradition: 'Then we wake up Christmas morning and throw rocks at trains.'
jokes.setup: Discussing Christmas traditions
jokes.punchline: Their cherished tradition is random destructive behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random and destructive - captures the show's dark absurdity in one perfect line

jokes.text: 'Why would grown men throw rocks at trains?' 'Why wouldn't we throw rocks at trains?'
jokes.setup: Dee questions the logic of their tradition
jokes.punchline: Mac's counter-question treats it as obviously normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - he genuinely doesn't understand why anyone would question this behavior

jokes.text: Frank's Christmas presents: 'He would find out whatever Christmas presents we wanted that year and he would buy 'em for himself instead of buying 'em for us.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why they hate Christmas
jokes.punchline: Frank's psychological warfare through gift-giving
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark parenting - shows Frank's systematic psychological cruelty in one perfect sentence

jokes.text: Frank crashes through the window: 'Goddamn it! Merry Christmas, bitches!'
jokes.setup: Mysterious crash sound building tension
jokes.punchline: Frank's dramatic entrance with cheerful profanity
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank - combining violence with holiday cheer, perfectly executed physical comedy entrance

jokes.text: Frank's fake-out with the designer bag: 'You wantee? Fake-out! It's mine.'
jokes.setup: Frank appears to offer Dee a nice gift
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it's another cruel tease
jokes.explanation: Establishes Frank's signature move - the casual cruelty is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Frank keeps snacks in designer bag: 'And I use it to keep in my Cheesy Loops and chocolate-covered malted milk balls.'
jokes.setup: Expensive designer bag being misused
jokes.punchline: Using luxury item to store cheap snacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank - has money but completely lacks taste or understanding of luxury

jokes.text: 'Do not speak ill of the dead.' 'She's speaking ill of you!'
jokes.setup: Frank defending Eugene Hamilton as dead
jokes.punchline: Dennis points out Frank is the one being insulted
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with economy - Frank's confusion becomes Dennis's opportunity for a zinger

jokes.text: Dee's reaction to bag misuse: 'Who puts cheesy snacks and chocolate in a designer bag? Oh, Dennis, he's gonna ruin the lining.'
jokes.setup: Frank's inappropriate use of luxury item
jokes.punchline: Dee's materialistic horror at the specific damage
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's superficial priorities - she's more upset about bag damage than the emotional cruelty

jokes.text: Eugene's religious conversion: 'I had a near-death experience and I saw the light and I saw the truth of the Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.'
jokes.setup: Gang expects Eugene wants revenge on Frank
jokes.punchline: He's become a born-again Christian who forgives everyone
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectations but becomes somewhat predictable - the gang's deflated reaction carries the humor

jokes.text: Gang's immediate disappointment: 'Oh. Uh-oh.' [Teeth click]
jokes.setup: Eugene reveals religious conversion
jokes.punchline: Their barely audible deflation
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - their dreams of revenge crushed in seconds, conveyed through minimal sound

jokes.text: Trying to manipulate Eugene: 'Frank's gotten nowhere near the Lord' and suggesting Frank will 'end up old and alone like yourself'
jokes.setup: Trying to convince religious Eugene to help them
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insulting him while asking for help
jokes.explanation: Classic gang manipulation - their selfishness makes them terrible at persuasion

jokes.text: Charlie finding karate trophy: 'I found that in the street.'
jokes.setup: Mac asks why Charlie has a karate trophy
jokes.punchline: Charlie just picked up random garbage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - his casual admission that he collects street garbage as trophies

jokes.text: Childhood Captain Caveman memory: 'Remember when we would shave the neighborhood dogs and glue the fur to our faces and pretend we were him?'
jokes.setup: Nostalgic childhood memory
jokes.punchline: Animal abuse as innocent fun
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny childhood 'innocence' - their twisted version of normal kid behavior

jokes.text: Charlie's follow-up: 'And then we would bash away at him and we'd bash the dogs to pieces.'
jokes.setup: Already disturbing dog-shaving story
jokes.punchline: Escalates to explicit violence
jokes.explanation: Escalates Mac's dark memory into pure horror while maintaining childlike enthusiasm

jokes.text: 'Goddamn it! Can I goddamn go? This is taking forever!' - young Mac on Christmas video
jokes.setup: Watching Mac's childhood Christmas video
jokes.punchline: Child Mac swearing impatiently on Christmas morning
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac was always impatient and profane, even as a child

jokes.text: Young Mac screaming about getting a Cabbage Patch Doll
jokes.setup: Christmas morning present opening
jokes.punchline: Mac's extreme excitement over a traditionally girly toy
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectations about masculine Mac's childhood - shows his current persona is constructed

jokes.text: Family appears during 'Christmas tradition': 'Who's down there?' 'Time to go, time to go!'
jokes.setup: Mac describes normal Christmas tradition
jokes.punchline: They're actually robbing houses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal - Mac's 'tradition' is actually breaking and entering, perfectly explains his twisted worldview

jokes.text: Mac's explanation: 'That was the Christmas tradition where you go from house to house collecting your presents.'
jokes.setup: Charlie questions what just happened
jokes.punchline: Mac genuinely believes burglary is a Christmas tradition
jokes.explanation: Shows how Mac's criminal father warped his understanding of normal traditions

jokes.text: 'That's why there were never any presents at my house when I got back. The neighbors took them.'
jokes.setup: Mac explains his Christmas tradition logic
jokes.punchline: He thinks his empty house proves reciprocal burglary
jokes.explanation: Mac's twisted logic creates a perfect closed loop that explains away his family's poverty

jokes.text: Charlie's reality check: 'Your dad is like a thief and a murderer who eats people, so he's not really trustworthy.'
jokes.setup: Mac defending his dad's Christmas tradition explanation
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually lists Mac's dad's horrific crimes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - matter-of-factly pointing out obvious red flags that Mac ignores

jokes.text: Frank's panic during ghost scene: 'Fire! Fire! Fire! We're gonna die!'
jokes.setup: Smoke machine meant to create ghostly atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately assumes building is burning down
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank paranoia - jumps to extreme conclusions and ruins their elaborate plan instantly

jokes.text: Dennis obsessing over the Countach: 'You do not make modifications on a Countach. That car is perfect.'
jokes.setup: Frank mentions modifying the sports car
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horror at the thought
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's materialistic obsessions - he cares more about the car than their plan

jokes.text: 'There's nothing in there.' 'Fake-out! There's nothing in any of 'em!'
jokes.setup: Children opening Christmas presents
jokes.punchline: All presents are empty boxes - ultimate fake-out
jokes.explanation: Devastating reveal - Frank's cruelty to children shown in full

jokes.text: Charlie's Santa tradition: Multiple Santas visiting his mom, giving him presents, then going upstairs to 'cheer her up'
jokes.setup: Charlie describes his wonderful Christmas tradition
jokes.punchline: Obvious description of his mother's prostitution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Charlie's innocent perspective on obvious adult situation creates perfect cringe

jokes.text: Charlie's confused memories: 'I didn't always understand the presents... one year, Santa even brought one of his elves.'
jokes.setup: Charlie recounting Christmas memories
jokes.punchline: References to adult toys and possibly underage workers
jokes.explanation: Continues Charlie's innocent narration of increasingly dark adult situations

jokes.text: Mac breaking it to Charlie: 'I think your mother was a prostitute.'
jokes.setup: Charlie's innocent Christmas story
jokes.punchline: Mac stating the obvious conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of what the audience already figured out - Mac as voice of reason

jokes.text: 'I'm fairly certain that those Santas were running a train on your mom for money.'
jokes.setup: Mac processing Charlie's story
jokes.punchline: Bluntly explains the reality to innocent Charlie
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty destroys Charlie's innocent childhood memories

jokes.text: 'They would just give my mom money and go in the-' [Bell dings] 'Let that settle in.'
jokes.setup: Charlie starting to defend his mom
jokes.punchline: Mid-sentence realization with bell punctuation
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - Charlie's dawning realization paired with sound effect creates perfect moment

jokes.text: Frank's couch hiding plan: 'That's what I do at home all the time- hide in the couch. I catch Charlie pounding off all the time.'
jokes.setup: Gang suggests hiding Frank to overhear conversations
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals his disturbing voyeuristic hobby
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disturbing Frank behavior - casual admission of spying on roommate

jokes.text: Dennis's horrified response: 'Why do you wanna catch Charlie masturbating? No, I don't care!'
jokes.setup: Frank's casual admission
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately regretting asking for clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to Frank's grossness - Dennis trying to maintain sanity in insane situation

jokes.text: Gang poses as consultants: 'We're consultationists.' 'We pop around here. We consult across the street too.'
jokes.setup: Office workers questioning their presence
jokes.punchline: Made-up job title and ridiculous coverage area
jokes.explanation: Classic gang incompetence - terrible improvised cover story getting worse as they talk

jokes.text: Frank naked in the couch: 'It's too hot in the couch.' 'Why are you naked, Frank?'
jokes.setup: Frank hidden inside couch
jokes.punchline: Emerges completely naked
jokes.explanation: Peak physical comedy - Frank's practical solution creates maximum chaos at office party

jokes.text: Charlie's coping mechanism: 'I buried it down deep. And two: I'm deciding I might not believe that that actually happened.'
jokes.setup: Mac asking if Charlie will flip out about his mom
jokes.punchline: Charlie's eerily rational coping strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie psychology - his surprising self-awareness and logical trauma response

jokes.text: Omnibot apology: 'When you were a little boy, I was stolen from you on Christmas day. It was a big misunderstanding. I miss you. I love you. I'm sorry.'
jokes.setup: Charlie and Mac visiting robbery victim to apologize
jokes.punchline: Robot delivering emotional apology
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - using the stolen robot to apologize for stealing it, perfectly Charlie solution

jokes.text: Finders keepers law: 'At this point, I believe the robot's falling under the finders-keepers law of America.'
jokes.setup: Ricky pointing out they stole the robot
jokes.punchline: Charlie's made-up legal justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - inventing legal precedent to justify keeping stolen goods

jokes.text: Mike Schmidt debate: 'Who is Mike Schmidt?' 'Who's Mike Schmidt! Have some respect here, man.'
jokes.setup: Mac using Philadelphia sports celebrity as leverage
jokes.punchline: Employee has no idea who he's talking about
jokes.explanation: Mac's Philadelphia pride creating impossible situation - his local heroes mean nothing elsewhere

jokes.text: Von Hayes reference: 'Next youre gonna tell me you don't know who Von Hayes is or Steve Bedrock Bedrosian.'
jokes.setup: Mac getting increasingly agitated about baseball knowledge
jokes.punchline: References to increasingly obscure Philadelphia players
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac obsession - escalating to players only hardcore Phillies fans would remember

jokes.text: Charlie confronting Santa: 'Did you fuck my mom?'
jokes.setup: Charlie sits on mall Santa's lap
jokes.punchline: Direct confrontation about mother's prostitution
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation - Charlie's trauma manifesting as public confrontation

jokes.text: Escalating Santa attack: 'Did you fuck my mom, Santa Claus? Did you fuck my fucking mom?'
jokes.setup: Charlie's initial question
jokes.punchline: Repeated aggressive questioning while attacking Santa
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Charlie's breakdown creating maximum public chaos

jokes.text: Frank's death philosophy: 'When I'm dead, just throw me in the trash.'
jokes.setup: Gang showing Frank his future grave
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual approach to death
jokes.explanation: Iconic Frank line - perfectly captures his nihilistic worldview in brutal economy

jokes.text: Dismissing Eugene: 'If you think Jesus and the Lord are so great, why dont you have them swing by in their Lamborghinis and take your ass home!'
jokes.setup: Eugene's constant religious references
jokes.punchline: Dee's frustrated materialistic dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee - combining religious mockery with materialistic obsession

jokes.text: Christmas elf vision: 'I heard you've been a grumpy-Gussy Christmas grouch.'
jokes.setup: Frank's mystical Christmas vision
jokes.punchline: Childish elf using baby talk
jokes.explanation: Generic Christmas parody - doesn't add much beyond visual absurdity

jokes.text: Elf's violent song about gouging eyes and chain-sawing knees
jokes.setup: Cute Christmas elf singing
jokes.punchline: Extremely graphic violence in sing-song style
jokes.explanation: Dark contrast humor - cute delivery of extreme violence, but feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: 'I didn't say the elf had no pants on.' 'Oh. I might have added something in my brain there at the end.'
jokes.setup: Frank describing his vision
jokes.punchline: Dennis admits to adding inappropriate details
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Dennis's mind going to inappropriate places

jokes.text: Gang's Christmas realization: 'The best part of Frank's story was how the four of us came together as a group to mercilessly slaughter him.'
jokes.setup: Looking for Christmas lesson in Frank's story
jokes.punchline: Finding unity through shared violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect gang logic - finding heartwarming meaning in shared cruelty, very true to character

jokes.text: Dismissing Frank: 'Oh, you go fuck yourself in your fat fuckin' ass.'
jokes.setup: Frank asking to join their Christmas celebration
jokes.punchline: Charlie's surprisingly harsh rejection
jokes.explanation: Unexpected from usually gentle Charlie - shows how much Frank has pushed them all

jokes.text: Caroling confrontation: 'We're trying to give you the Christmas spirit, dick-hole!' 'I don't want it!' 'You're gonna get it anyway!'
jokes.setup: Gang trying to spread Christmas cheer
jokes.punchline: Forcing Christmas spirit through aggression
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction - spreading joy through hostility, captures gang's backwards approach

jokes.text: Eugene's gun reveal: 'You stole a lot of money from me. You wrecked my career. You left me all alone to rot in a shit-hole apartment. Now it's time for payback!'
jokes.setup: Eugene appearing to be reformed Christian
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's still vengeful and armed
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - Eugene's fake conversion revealed, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: 'What happened to all the God stuff?' 'I'm still a liar and a thief.'
jokes.setup: Questioning Eugene's religious transformation
jokes.punchline: Eugene admits he never changed
jokes.explanation: Clean punchline - Eugene's honest admission of continued dishonesty has good irony

jokes.text: Snow machine failure: 'That ain't gonna work. Yeah, it's from the '70s, dick. It's not gonna work.'
jokes.setup: Eugene trying to use the snow machine for dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Machine is broken junk as expected
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback - their junk purchase fails at the worst moment for Eugene

jokes.text: Traditional rock throwing: 'Just throw rocks at trains. You're gonna feel better. That's it! Merry Christmas, everyone!'
jokes.setup: Episode ending with gang together
jokes.punchline: Return to their destructive Christmas tradition
jokes.explanation: Perfect circular ending - returns to opening absurdist tradition as their genuine bonding
